preamble: The shift from the voiced bilabial stop [b] to the vowel [u] is a form of vocalization. It typically occurs in syllable-final (coda) positions or between vowels, where the consonant weakens through a fricative stage [β] before losing its consonantal nature entirely.

examples.note: Coda /b/ before /s/ vocalized to /u/, yielding the diphthong 'au'.
Intervocalic /b/ spirantized to [β] then vocalized to [u], yielding diphthong [ew].

sources: Williams, E. B. (1938). From Latin to Portuguese: Historical Phonology and Morphology of the Portuguese Language. University of Pennsylvania Press.
